title: Mencius 1A.7: The Ox Mountain Parable
description: A mountain stripped bare still has shoots of goodness — like human nature, degraded by circumstance but never destroyed.
introduction: A mountain stripped bare still has shoots of goodness — like human nature, degraded by circumstance but never destroyed.

translation.text: The trees of Ox Mountain were once beautiful. But axes came morning after morning. Can the mountain remain beautiful?
